text.content:
When this set is chosen, every Vampyre in the scene gains +3 to checks made towards feats of acrobatics, such as gymnastics, parkour maneuvers, or certain martial arts maneuvers. After they make such a check or use one of the benefits below, they may dodge bullets using their physical skill until the start of their next turn.

Mook Options:

  • 1 Point, in place of movement on the Vampyre's turn: Relying on ropes and counterweights, the Vampyre is able to move up to 20 spaces (40 m/yds) in place of their normal movement, this turn. When so doing, they may ignore vertical movement (but not walls, if they're enclosed) They may still take their action in the middle of this movement.

  • 2 Points, as an action during their turn: Catching an enemy in their web of ropes, the Vampyre and that enemy switch places on the map if they are within 10 spaces (20m/yds) of one another. The Vampyre may make one attack with an equipped melee weapon against this target as they swap places (can be dodged as normal). After landing, the enemy must succeed a DV15 DEX Athletics check (physical for mooks) - on failure, they fall prone.

  • 3 Points, after successfully executing a grapple action: Instead of being grappled by the Vampyre, the target of the grapple is hoisted 10 m/yds into the air, and hang there upside down. It's assumed that the target retains their weapons or other items they were holding, but must succeed a DV13 DEX Contortionist, DV17 BODY Athletics, or DV19 shoulder arms/heavy weapons/handgun/etc. check to break free. Until then, they swing lightly from side to side, cannot otherwise move, and all actions they take (including those to free themselves) are made at a -4 penalty. Their allies may shoot the rope by making an aimed shot 1 meter above the enemy's location.
